steps.title: Look for x-intercepts
steps.content: Values of $x$ where $f(x)=0$ are the $x$-intercepts of the graph. So the task is to count how many times the graph touches or crosses the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Use the graph position
steps.content: The graph shown stays below the $x$-axis for all displayed values and approaches a horizontal asymptote below the $x$-axis. It never reaches $y=0$.

steps.title: Count the zeros
steps.content: Because the graph has no $x$-intercepts, there are zero values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. The answer is D.
